entry.title: sui_v1.72.2_1779055433_release: rpc: map simulate user errors to invalid argument (#26560) (#26675)
sui_v1.73.0_1778907891_ci: sui-types: convert ValidDuring TransactionExpiration from proto (#26670)
entry.content:
Previously, TryFrom<&proto::TransactionExpiration> rejected the
ValidDuring variant with an "unknown TransactionExpirationKind" error,
so callers that supplied a ValidDuring expiration to gRPC endpoints
like SimulateTransaction were unable to use it even though the forward
conversion already encoded the variant.

With this commit, the TryFrom decodes ValidDuring by parsing the
chain field back into a ChainIdentifier, decoding the optional
timestamps via a new timestamp_to_ms helper, and requiring both
chain and nonce to be present. Also add e2e tests next to the
simulate/resolve tests: a round-trip test over the conversion and a
sim_test that supplies an explicit ValidDuring expiration to
SimulateTransaction and verifies the resolved transaction preserves
epoch, min_epoch, chain, and nonce both in the proto response
and after BCS deserialization to TransactionData.

entry.title: sui_v1.73.0_1778884533_ci: [easy][adapter] Remove a bunch of old dead code (#26668)
entry.content:
Description

Was dead and no longer used. So remove it.

Test plan

It was dead code.

Release notes

your changes, release notes aren't required.

that describes the impact of your changes that a user might notice and
any actions they must take to implement updates.

entry.title: sui_v1.73.0_1778851689_ci: [move][move-decompiler] Emit loop bodies in reverse post-order (#26644)
entry.content:
Description

Today, insert_breaks has a LatchKind::InLoop case that drops a
Jump on the assumption that its target is the next sibling in the
emitted Seq. With sort-by-NodeIndex, that's true only because
move_stackless_bytecode_2 happens to lay blocks out in CFG-flow order.
Nothing asserts that invariant. If upstream ever does something
different, sort-order could disagree with CFG-flow and the Jump drop
would silently produce wrong fall-through. Instead, we reverse
post-order traverse the loop_nodes to ensure the correct order: the
IfElse's post-dom is genuinely the next sibling regardless of how blocks
are numbered.

Test plan

All 52 snapshot tests pass. Zero snapshot drift — confirms RPO and
sort-order agree on the current corpus, which is the predicted outcome.
The change buys robustness if upstream ever renumbers blocks.

Release notes

Check each box that your changes affect. If none of the boxes relate to
your changes, release notes aren't required.

For each box you select, include information after the relevant heading
that describes the impact of your changes that a user might notice and
any actions they must take to implement updates.

  • Protocol:

  • GraphQL:
  • CLI:
  • Rust SDK:
  • Indexing Framework:

MystenLabs/sui-rust-sdk#256 lands.

Co-authored-by: Brandon Williams brandon@mystenlabs.com

entry.title: sui_v1.73.0_1778776992_ci: docs: fix details dropdown arrow and expand animation (#26624)
entry.content:
Summary

  • Removed custom arrow pill (→ in blue rounded square) that conflicted
    with Docusaurus's built-in border-triangle chevron
  • Removed slideDown keyframe animation that fought Docusaurus's
    Collapsible height animation, causing a visible upward shift at end of
    expand
  • Changed container transition: all to only animate border-color and
    box-shadow (not layout properties)
  • Removed instant margin-bottom/padding-bottom/border-bottom on
    open summary that caused layout shift during expand
  • Set arrow color via --docusaurus-details-decoration-color CSS
    variable for both light and dark mode

Test plan

  • Open a page with <details> dropdowns (e.g., any reference page)
  • Verify arrow is visible and rotates smoothly on open/close
  • Verify expand animation is seamless with no upward shift
  • Check both light and dark mode

🤖 Generated with Claude Code

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) noreply@anthropic.com

entry.title: sui_v1.73.0_1778755228_ci: [decompiler] Total post-dominators over divergent regions (#26615)
entry.content:
Description

structure_acyclic_region asks petgraph for the immediate
post-dominator of a node and unwraps it. petgraph's simple_fast leaves
immediate_dominator undefined for any node not reachable from the
dominator-tree root — and in the reversed CFG rooted at return_,
that's exactly the input nodes that never reach a sink in the original
(infinite loops with no exit, abort-only regions).

This make post-domination total over the input by adding impossible
edges: after wiring return_ to original sinks, compute SCCs of the
original CFG and, for each terminal SCC (one whose outgoing edges all
stay inside the SCC), add return_ -> highest-rank-input-node-in-SCC.
Singleton-sink SCCs slip through the check vacuously and get a redundant
edge, but simple_fast handles the duplicate fine.

Test plan

Adds three structuring unit tests for the shapes that exercised the fix
(lone self-loop; if/else that converges and then falls into a trailing
infinite loop; if/else whose two arms each fall into a separate infinite
loop, producing two disjoint terminal SCCs).

Release notes

Check each box that your changes affect. If none of the boxes relate to
your changes, release notes aren't required.

For each box you select, include information after the relevant heading
that describes the impact of your changes that a user might notice and
any actions they must take to implement updates.

  • Nodes (Validators and Full nodes):

  • JSON-RPC:
  • GraphQL:
  • CLI:
  • Rust SDK:
  • Indexing Framework:

Co-authored-by: Sam Blackshear sam@mystenlabs.com
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.5 noreply@anthropic.com
